title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement andreas@gdamsbo.dk. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Get started
text: DAH (Days‑‑Home) pipeline takes long‑format table clinical events calculates, patient, many days user‑defined window (default 30 days) spent home. Primary admission → start window.Hospital rehabilitation stays → days home. patient dies inside window, DAH set 0. column names can customised; need tell wrapper columns correspond required concepts.

what: 1️⃣ Get the package and load the example data set
title: Get started
text: package ships reproducible synthetic data set called synthetic_data. data already canonical column names pipeline expects: patient_id event_id event_type ("primary", "hospital", "rehabilitation" "death") start_date, end_date intervention_date (filled primary admission) death_date (filled rows type "death")
code: pak::pak("agdamsbo/dahcalc") library(dahcalc) str(dahcalc::synthetic_data) #> tibble [131 × 7] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame) #>  $ patient_id       : int [1:131] 1 1 1 2 2 2 2 2 3 3 ... #>  $ event_id         : int [1:131] 1 2 3 1 2 3 4 5 1 2 ... #>  $ event_type       : chr [1:131] "primary" "rehabilitation" "rehabilitation" "primary" ... #>  $ start_date       : Date[1:131], format: "2023-08-09" "2023-08-20" ... #>  $ end_date         : Date[1:131], format: "2023-08-16" "2023-08-30" ... #>  $ intervention_date: Date[1:131], format: "2023-08-13" NA ... #>  $ death_date       : Date[1:131], format: NA NA ...

what: 2️⃣ (Optional) Rename columns to match your own file
title: Get started
text: CSV uses different names can rename calling pipeline. demonstration rename lower‑case scheme, skip step entirely file already uses canonical names. Now my_dt looks like data user might supply.
code: # Make a copy so we do not alter the original object my_dt <- dahcalc::synthetic_data  # Suppose the user’s file uses the following names: #  pid, eid, etype, adm_start, adm_end, int_date, dod my_dt <- setNames(   my_dt,   c("pid", "eid", "etype", "adm_start",           "adm_end", "int_date", "dod") )  head(my_dt) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>     pid   eid etype          adm_start  adm_end    int_date   dod    #>   <int> <int> <chr>          <date>     <date>     <date>     <date> #> 1     1     1 primary        2023-08-09 2023-08-16 2023-08-13 NA     #> 2     1     2 rehabilitation 2023-08-20 2023-08-30 NA         NA     #> 3     1     3 rehabilitation 2023-09-01 2023-09-09 NA         NA     #> 4     2     1 primary        2023-05-01 2023-05-12 2023-05-05 NA     #> 5     2     2 rehabilitation 2023-05-13 2023-05-17 NA         NA     #> 6     2     3 rehabilitation 2023-05-22 2023-06-01 NA         NA

title: Get started
text: Tell wrapper names using (skip arguments kept canonical names). res$per_patient – one row per patient DAH values. res$cohort_summary – aggregated statistics whole cohort. res$plot – ggplot object showing DAH distribution.
code: res <- run_dah_pipeline(   data_long             = my_dt,   window_days           = 30,        # 30‑day observation window   patient_id_col        = "pid",   event_id_col          = "eid",   event_type_col        = "etype",   start_date_col        = "adm_start",   end_date_col          = "adm_end",   intervention_date_col = "int_date",   death_date_col        = "dod",   verbose               = TRUE,      # prints a short validation summary   keep_original_names   = TRUE        # keep the user’s column names in the output ) #>  #> === Validation summary === #>    patient_id overlap #> 1           1   FALSE #> 2           2   FALSE #> 3           3   FALSE #> 4           4   FALSE #> 5           5   FALSE #> 6           6   FALSE #> 7           7   FALSE #> 8           8   FALSE #> 9 str(res, max.level = 1) #> List of 5 #>  $ per_patient   :'data.frame':  40 obs. of  7 variables: #>  $ cohort_summary:'data.frame':  1 obs. of  10 variables: #>  $ plot          : <ggplot2::ggplot> title: Get started
text: Typical output shows: number patients, mean / median / SD DAH, percentage full 30 days home, DAH per 100 person‑days, etc.
code: print(res$cohort_summary) #>     window_days n_patients mean_dah median_dah sd_dah q25_dah q75_dah #> 25%          30         40    18.35       17.5   6.62   12.75   23.25 #>     pct_full_home mean_effective_window_days dah_per_100_pt_days #> 25%           7.5                         30               61.17

title: Get started
text: Key columns ’ll see:

title: Get started
text: histogram lets quickly see many patients spent 30 days home, many long institutional stays, deaths fall.
code: print(res$plot)   # displays a histogram with a vertical line at 30 days

what: 7️⃣ What to do with your own data
title: Get started
text: real CSV file, workflow : ’s need obtain Days‑‑Home metrics data set follows required event structure.
code: my_real_data <- read.csv("my_clinical_file.csv")  # (optional) rename columns if they differ from the canonical ones # setnames(my_real_data, old = "...", new = "...")  result <- run_dah_pipeline(   dt_long               = my_real_data,   patient_id_col        = "my_id",   event_id_col          = "my_event_id",   event_type_col        = "my_type",   start_date_col        = "my_start",   end_date_col          = "my_end",   intervention_date_col = "my_intervention",   death_date_col        = "my_death",   window_days           = 30,   keep_original_names   = TRUE )  # Then explore result$cohort_summary, result$per_patient, result$plot …

title: Authors and Citation
text: Andreas Gammelgaard Damsbo. Author, maintainer. Kim Morgenstjerne Ørskov. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Damsbo , Ørskov K (2026). dahcalc: Calculate Days--Home. R package version 2026.7.1, https://github.com/agdamsbo/dahcalc.
code: @Manual{,   title = {dahcalc: Calculate Days-at-Home},   author = {Andreas Gammelgaard Damsbo and Kim Morgenstjerne Ørskov},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 2026.7.1},   url = {https://github.com/agdamsbo/dahcalc}, }

title: Calculate Days-at-Home
text: R reference pipeline Days--Home metric oulined : https://github.com/monash-neurointervention/uia-dah30 still work progress.

title: Calculate Days-at-Home
text: Use pak get package GitHub: package matured, heading CRAN.
code: pak::pak("agdamsbo/dahcalc") library(dahcalc)

title: Calculate Days-at-Home
text: Please note dahcalc project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

what: Add observation window and death‑inside‑window flag. — apply_window_and_death
title: Add observation window and death‑inside‑window flag. — apply_window_and_death
text: Add observation window death‑inside‑window flag.

title: Add observation window and death‑inside‑window flag. — apply_window_and_death
code: apply_window_and_death(   primary_data,   window_days = 30L,   intervention_date_col = "intervention_date",   death_date_col = "death_date" )

title: Add observation window and death‑inside‑window flag. — apply_window_and_death
text: primary_data data.frame `extract_primary`. window_days integer length observation window (default = 30). intervention_date_col column name intervention date. death_date_col column name death date.

title: Add observation window and death‑inside‑window flag. — apply_window_and_death
text: `primary_data` `window_start`, `window_end`,   `died_in_window` columns.

title: Compute Days‑At‑Home per patient. — compute_dah
text: Compute Days‑‑Home per patient.

title: Compute Days‑At‑Home per patient. — compute_dah
code: compute_dah(win_data, inst_counts, window_days = 30L)

title: Compute Days‑At‑Home per patient. — compute_dah
text: win_data output `apply_window_and_death`. inst_counts output `count_institutional_days`. window_days length observation window (default = 30).

title: Compute Days‑At‑Home per patient. — compute_dah
text: Data.frame `patient_id`, `dah`,   `effective_window`, `institutional_days`, `died_in_window`.

what: Count institutional days inside the window. — count_institutional_days
title: Count institutional days inside the window. — count_institutional_days
text: Count institutional days inside window.

title: Count institutional days inside the window. — count_institutional_days
code: count_institutional_days(inst_data, win_data)

title: Count institutional days inside the window. — count_institutional_days
text: inst_data data.frame merged intervals (`patient_id`, `start`, `end`). win_data data.frame `apply_window_and_death` (must contain `patient_id`, `window_start`, `window_end`).

title: Count institutional days inside the window. — count_institutional_days
text: Data.frame `patient_id`, `institutional_days`.

title: Extract hospital / rehabilitation stays. — extract_institutional
text: Extract hospital / rehabilitation stays.

title: Extract hospital / rehabilitation stays. — extract_institutional
code: extract_institutional(   data,   patient_id_col = "patient_id",   event_type_col = "event_type",   start_date_col = "start_date",   end_date_col = "end_date" )

title: Extract hospital / rehabilitation stays. — extract_institutional
text: data validated data.frame. patient_id_col column name patient ID. event_type_col column name event type. start_date_col column name start date. end_date_col column name end date.

title: Extract hospital / rehabilitation stays. — extract_institutional
text: Data.frame `patient_id`, `start`, `end` (integer days).

title: Extract the primary admission. — extract_primary
text: Extract primary admission.

title: Extract the primary admission. — extract_primary
code: extract_primary(   data,   patient_id_col = "patient_id",   event_type_col = "event_type",   intervention_date_col = "intervention_date",   death_date_col = "death_date" )

title: Extract the primary admission. — extract_primary
text: data validated data.frame. patient_id_col column name patient ID (default `"patient_id"`). event_type_col column name event type (default `"event_type"`). intervention_date_col column name intervention date (default `"intervention_date"`). death_date_col column name death date (default `"death_date"`).

title: Extract the primary admission. — extract_primary
text: Data.frame `patient_id`, `intervention_date`, `death_date`.

title: Merge overlapping / adjacent intervals. — merge_overlaps
text: Merge overlapping / adjacent intervals.

title: Merge overlapping / adjacent intervals. — merge_overlaps
code: merge_overlaps(inst_data)

title: Merge overlapping / adjacent intervals. — merge_overlaps
text: inst_data data.frame `patient_id`, `start`, `end`.

title: Merge overlapping / adjacent intervals. — merge_overlaps
text: Data.frame merged intervals per patient.

text: Plot DAH distribution.

text: `ggplot2` object.

what: Run the whole DAH pipeline. — run_dah_pipeline
title: Run the whole DAH pipeline. — run_dah_pipeline
text: Accepts long‑format data.frame optional column‑name mappings.

title: Run the whole DAH pipeline. — run_dah_pipeline
code: run_dah_pipeline(   data_long,   window_days = 30L,   patient_id_col = "patient_id",   event_id_col = "event_id",   event_type_col = "event_type",   start_date_col = "start_date",   end_date_col = "end_date",   intervention_date_col = "intervention_date",   death_date_col = "death_date",   verbose = TRUE,   keep_original_names = FALSE )

title: Run the whole DAH pipeline. — run_dah_pipeline
text: data_long raw data (`data.frame` `data.table`). window_days observation window length (default = 30). patient_id_col column name patient ID (default `"patient_id"`). event_id_col column name event ID (default `"event_id"`). event_type_col column name event type (default `"event_type"`). start_date_col column name start date (default `"start_date"`). end_date_col column name end date (default `"end_date"`). intervention_date_col column name intervention date (default `"intervention_date"`). death_date_col column name death date (default `"death_date"`). verbose logical; print overlapping‑stay summary (default = TRUE). keep_original_names logical; TRUE, keep user‑supplied column names per‑patient output.

title: Run the whole DAH pipeline. — run_dah_pipeline
text: List `per_patient`, `cohort_summary`, `plot`,   `overlap_flag`, `column_mapping`.

what: Summarise DAH at the cohort level. — summarise_cohort
title: Summarise DAH at the cohort level. — summarise_cohort
text: Summarise DAH cohort level.

title: Summarise DAH at the cohort level. — summarise_cohort
code: summarise_cohort(data, window_days = 30L)

title: Summarise DAH at the cohort level. — summarise_cohort
text: data per‑patient result `compute_dah`. window_days observation window length (default = 30).

title: Summarise DAH at the cohort level. — summarise_cohort
text: One‑row data.frame summary statistics.

what: Example synthetic long‑format clinical data — synthetic_data
title: Example synthetic long‑format clinical data — synthetic_data
text: reproducible, synthetic data set mimics structure required **DAH** (Days‑‑Home) analysis pipeline.  data *long* format contain columns: patient_id Integer identifier patient. event_id Consecutive integer event per patient. event_type Character; one `"primary"` (first admission),                     `"hospital"` (sub‑sequent admission),                     `"rehabilitation"` `"death"`. start_date Date start event (NA death rows). end_date Date end event (NA death rows). intervention_date Date primary clinical intervention.                     non‑NA rows event_type == "primary". death_date Date death; NA patients survive                     observation period.  death rows                     actual date death; rows NA. data contain **40** simulated patients.  Approximately 30  patients separate death row; remaining patients survive 45‑day observation horizon.  Hospital rehabilitation stays randomly generated, may overlap, limited maximum 45 days primary admission.

title: Example synthetic long‑format clinical data — synthetic_data
text: **data.frame** 131 rows 7 columns (see description ).

title: Example synthetic long‑format clinical data — synthetic_data
text: Generated internally package using script   `data-raw/generate-synthetic_data.R`.  random seed set `2026`   reproducibility.

title: Example synthetic long‑format clinical data — synthetic_data
code: ## Load the data set data(synthetic_data, package = "dahcalc")  ## Quick look at the first few rows head(synthetic_data) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   patient_id event_id event_type     start_date end_date   intervention_date #>        <int>    <int> <chr>          <date>     <date>     <date>            #> 1          1        1 primary        2023-08-09 2023-08-16 2023-08-13        #> 2          1        2 rehabilitation 2023-08-20 2023-08-30 NA                #> 3          1        3 rehabilitation 2023-09-01 2023-09-09 NA                #> 4          2        1 primary        2023-05-01 2023-05-12 2023-05-05        #> 5          2        2 rehabilitation 2023-05-13 2023-05-17 NA                #> 6          2        3 rehabilitation 2023-05-22 2023-06-01 NA                #> # ℹ 1 more variable: death_date <date>  ## Run the DAH pipeline on the synthetic data result <- run_dah_pipeline(synthetic_data, window_days = 30) #>  #> === Validation summary === #>    patient_id overlap #> 1           1   FALSE #> 2           2   FALSE #> 3           3   FALSE #> 4           4   FALSE #> 5           5   FALSE #> 6           6   FALSE #> 7           7   FALSE #> 8           8   FALSE #> 9           9   FALSE #> 10         10   FALSE #> 11         11   FALSE #> 12         12   FALSE #> 13         13   FALSE #> 14         14   FALSE #> 15         15   FALSE #> 16         16   FALSE #> 17         18   FALSE #> 18         19   FALSE #> 19         20   FALSE #> 20         21   FALSE #> 21         22   FALSE #> 22         23   FALSE #> 23         24   FALSE #> 24         25   FALSE #> 25         26   FALSE #> 26         27   FALSE #> 27         28   FALSE #> 28         29   FALSE #> 29         30   FALSE #> 30         31   FALSE #> 31         32   FALSE #> 32         33   FALSE #> 33         34   FALSE #> 34         36   FALSE #> 35         37   FALSE #> 36         39   FALSE #> 37         40   FALSE #> 38         NA   FALSE result$cohort_summary #>     window_days n_patients mean_dah median_dah sd_dah q25_dah q75_dah #> 25%          30         40    13.03       13.5   9.52       6   20.25 #>     pct_full_home mean_effective_window_days dah_per_100_pt_days #> 25%             5                       26.9               48.42

what: Validate the long‑format event data. — validate_events
title: Validate the long‑format event data. — validate_events
text: Ensures required columns exist, Dates, patient exactly one primary event, start ≤ end, flags overlapping hospital/rehab stays (report‑).

title: Validate the long‑format event data. — validate_events
code: validate_events(   data,   patient_id_col = "patient_id",   event_id_col = "event_id",   event_type_col = "event_type",   start_date_col = "start_date",   end_date_col = "end_date",   intervention_date_col = "intervention_date",   death_date_col = "death_date" )

title: Validate the long‑format event data. — validate_events
text: data `data.frame` containing events. patient_id_col column name patient ID (default `"patient_id"`). event_id_col column name event ID (default `"event_id"`). event_type_col column name event type (default `"event_type"`). start_date_col column name start date (default `"start_date"`). end_date_col column name end date (default `"end_date"`). intervention_date_col column name primary intervention date (default `"intervention_date"`). death_date_col column name death date (default `"death_date"`).

title: Validate the long‑format event data. — validate_events
text: List `valid_data` (copy input) `overlap_flag`   (data.frame: `patient_id`, `overlap` logical).
